Bash(# Now let''s understand the full picture - look at what the screenshots show # by checking file sizes \(larger = more complex/colorful content\) # and examining the DOM one more time # Check the key screenshots ls -la /d/Projects/2026/sonic-website/.playwright-mcp/inspect-{01,02,03,10,11,12,13,14,15,20,30,31,32,33}*.png)
Bash(# Check if animejs is installed ls /d/Projects/2026/sonic-website/node_modules/animejs/)

Bash(# Check if animate\(\) from animejs v4 supports translateX/translateY directly # or if it needs to use CSS transforms differently grep -n ""translateX\\|translateY\\|transform"" /d/Projects/2026/sonic-website/node_modules/animejs/dist/modules/index.js)

Bash(# Let''s check if animejs v4 supports translateX as a shorthand grep -r ""translateX\\|\\""x\\""\\|keyframes"" /d/Projects/2026/sonic-website/node_modules/animejs/dist/modules/animation/animation.cjs)

Bash(# Let me check if animejs v4 transforms use x/y instead of translateX/Y grep -r ""shorthand\\|transform.*x\\|\\""x\\""\\|\\""y\\""\\|translateX"" /d/Projects/2026/sonic-website/node_modules/animejs/dist/modules/core/styles.cjs)

Bash(# animejs v4 uses the same transform property names # Let me check the actual issue - the inline transform in the CSS is ""translate\(-50%,-50%\)"" # but animejs v4 might use CSS individual transforms \(translateX, translateY as CSS properties\) # vs the old anime.js v3 which used inline transform string manipulation # Key question: does animejs v4 animate the CSS ''transform'' property or individual ''translateX'' CSS prop? grep ""translateX\\|individual\\|css.*transform\\|transform.*css"" /d/Projects/2026/sonic-website/node_modules/animejs/dist/modules/core/styles.cjs)
